body.title: CLOSED: swarm tmux face — JSONL transcript, capture-pane, send-keys attribution
body.result: JSONL transcript reader + capture-pane + send-keys attribution; 21 tests; commit 397de34
body.description:
Implement the tmux transport face: outbound uses capture-pane (preferring CC's JSONL transcripts at ~/.claude/projects/ over raw capture-pane for escape-code-free output) to produce bus envelopes. Inbound takes bus envelopes and injects into the agent's tmux session via send-keys with attribution prefix so the agent knows the message is not from the primary user.

**Affected files:** agent_datacenter/devices/claude/shim.py (extend with tmux transport), new agent_datacenter/devices/claude/tmux_face.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite
**Scope boundary:** IN — outbound: JSONL transcript reader + capture-pane fallback → envelope; inbound: envelope → send-keys with attribution. OUT — web UI rendering; channel subscription (T-swarm-channel-mechanism); ACL/mute.
**Test plan:** unit tests with mock subprocess — outbound: JSONL file with 2 turns → 2 envelopes emitted; capture-pane fallback fires when JSONL absent; inbound: envelope with sender=igor → send-keys called with `igor: <message>` prefix.
**Pass condition:** Given a mock JSONL transcript, the tmux face emits correctly-shaped bus envelopes; given an inbound envelope, `tmux send-keys` is called with attribution so the receiving agent can distinguish injected messages from primary user input.
